description: STT on options sale increased to 0.0625% on premium (was on notional)
SEBI revised ESM framework (Enhanced Surveillance Measure) — new entry/exit criteria for illiquid stocks
Removed 16 stocks from F&O after failing eligibility criteria (BANDHANBNK, CANBK, etc.) — quarter sigma cycle
STT futures 0.0125%→0.02% (60% hike); STT options on premium 0.0625%→0.1% (60% hike). Budget 2024 announcement.
Last BANKNIFTY weekly contract expiry — weekly options discontinued for BANKNIFTY
Single weekly expiry per exchange — NSE keeps NIFTY only; BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY/NIFTYNXT50 weekly options stopped. Major flow redistribution.
BANKNIFTY lot size 15→30 (existing quarterly/half-yearly contracts cutover)
NIFTY lot size 25→75 (3x increase; existing quarterly/half-yearly contracts cutover). Major retail-flow shift to cash segment.
Full option premium upfront — no leverage on long options. SEBI ends option-buying via reduced margin.
Intraday position snapshots — minimum 4 random snapshots per session for cash + F&O
T+1 margin collection (was T+2). Brokers must collect all margins by next working day except VaR + ELM.

SEBI tightens MWPL formula: min(15% free float, 65× avg cash volume) — most mid-caps see MWPL cut 50-80%. Single-stock position limits: individual 10%, prop 20%, FPI/broker 30%. New FutEq OI methodology.
SEBI implementation of derivative eligibility for non-benchmark indices. Nifty Bank, Nifty Financial Services methodology revised.
Intraday MWPL/FutEq OI monitoring — 4 random checks per session. Stocks can enter+exit ban intraday.
STT futures 0.02%→0.05% (150% hike); STT options on premium 0.1%→0.15% (50% hike). Budget 2026 announcement.
6 stocks added to F&O segment (per quarter-sigma March 2026 cycle)
